name: Kofta Noor Jahani
recipeInstructions:
1. Grate the boiled potatoes and grate the paneer. Add both to the grated potatoes.
2. Mix in the finely chopped green chillies, salt, and cornflour. Mix well and form small round balls.
3. Grate the carrots and mix the raisins into them. Make a small indentation in each potato ball and stuff with some of the carrot and raisin mixture. Seal the potato ball tightly, ensuring no holes remain. Deep fry all the balls until golden brown. Remove and set aside.
4. Gravy:
- Heat oil in a heavy-based pan. Add the boiled onion paste and cook until the onions are lightly pink.
- Add the ginger and garlic paste and sauté for a moment.
- Stir in turmeric, chili powder, and cook until well mixed with the paste.
- Add the cashew nut paste and cook, stirring frequently to prevent sticking.
- Pour in about 1/2 cup of water and simmer on low heat for 10 minutes. Season with salt to taste.
- Toss in the fried koftas and gently mix.
- Finish with a drizzle of fresh cream (optional) and garnish with chopped coriander. Serve hot with naan or rice.
This dish was reportedly created for the beautiful queen Noor Jahan, though its origins are uncertain. Regardless, it is delicious!
